success_surface: Iterative debugging with defensive coding patterns led to robust spreadsheet manipulation
effective_behaviors: Pre-convert mutable collection references to immutable lists before iteration to avoid runtime modification errors
Use direct cell access methods rather than intermediate data structures when row/column dimensions may vary
Implement comprehensive null/empty value detection covering multiple data types
Add post-processing verification that reloads the output file to confirm transformations
Employ conditional propagation logic with state reset triggers at boundary conditions
Use iterative debug printing to trace execution flow during multi-step transformations
evidence: First failed attempt: TypeError from ws.unmerge_cells() requiring int argument instead of string range
Second failed attempt: RuntimeError 'Set changed size during iteration' when looping over ws.merged_cells.ranges
Third failed attempt: IndexError from accessing tuple indices beyond available column count
Successful attempt: Converting merged_ranges to list() before iteration prevented mutation errors
Successful attempt: Switched from pre-loaded data tuples to direct ws.cell(row, col) calls for robustness
Successful attempt: Comprehensive empty check using 'is None or (isinstance(...str...) and strip() == "")'
Successful attempt: Verification section reloaded output file and printed sample rows to confirm correctness

memory_items.title: Safe Collection Iteration
memory_items.lesson: openpyxl collection objects like merged_cells.ranges can mutate during iteration; always snapshot to list()
memory_items.generalization: When iterating over workbook collection attributes that may change, convert to list immediately: list(collection.attribute) to prevent RuntimeError during loop execution

memory_items.title: Direct Cell Access Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Loading entire row ranges into tuples fails when some rows have fewer columns; use per-cell access instead
memory_items.generalization: For irregular spreadsheets, query individual cells via worksheet.cell(row, column) within loops rather than batch-reading variable-length row arrays

memory_items.title: Robust Empty Detection
memory_items.lesson: Empty cells can be None or empty strings with whitespace; handle both cases explicitly
memory_items.generalization: Check for empty values using combined condition: value is None or (value is string and value.strip() == '') to catch all edge cases uniformly

memory_items.title: Output Verification Workflow
memory_items.lesson: Saving doesn't guarantee correct transformation; reload and verify key rows after write
memory_items.generalization: After saving workbook modifications, reopen the output file and print sample cell values to validate changes applied as expected before concluding

memory_items.title: Stateful Propagation with Reset Triggers
memory_items.lesson: Forward-fill operations need clear stop conditions tied to other column states
memory_items.generalization: When propagating values across rows, maintain a current state variable and reset it at boundary markers identified in related columns to control propagation scope

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Safe Workbook Object Iteration
- **Critical**: Convert openpyxl collection objects to lists before iterating (e.g., `list(ws.merged_cells.ranges)`). These collections may mutate during iteration causing `RuntimeError: Set changed size during iteration`.
- When modifying merged cells or similar collections, always snapshot first, then iterate over the copy.

### Variable Row Structure Handling
- Do not assume uniform row lengths when reading data programmatically. Batch reads (`[cell.value for cell in row]`) fail with `IndexError` on irregular sheets.
- Preferred pattern: Query individual cells via `ws.cell(row=row_num, column=column_num)` inside iteration loops to handle sparse or varying column counts gracefully.

### Comprehensive Empty Cell Detection
- Empty values appear as either `None` OR whitespace-only strings.
- Use combined check: `value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() == '')` for consistent empty detection across all data types.

### Post-Save Verification Protocol
- Never assume saved output is correct without verification.
- Reload the output file after `wb.save()`, then print representative sample rows/columns to confirm transformations.
- Include verification code as part of the standard workflow, not optional.

### Stateful Data Propagation
- Forward-fill or propagate operations require explicit boundary detection.
- Maintain a `current_state` variable; reset it when hitting trigger conditions (e.g., another column becomes empty).
- Track state transitions with debug logging to identify where propagation starts/stops.
